title: [$250] [Sentry] fix The database connection is closing issue

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Bug
labels.description: Something is broken. Auto assigns a BugZero manager.

body:
Sentry issue https://expensify.sentry.io/issues/7092239780 Thread https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C05LX9D6E07/p1770496102994319 ### Please re-state the problem that we are trying to solve in this issue. When Onyx operations attempt to create an IndexedDB transaction, the browser may have already closed the database connection, causing: InvalidStateError: Failed to execute 'transaction' on 'IDBDatabase': The database connection is closing. This affects ~58,700 occurrences across 430 users on all browsers (Chrome: 31K, Safari: 18K, Firefox: 5K, Mobile Safari: 3K, Edge: 1K). The error is an unhandled promise rejection. ### What is the root cause of that problem? The existing db.onclose handler (line 22) sets dbp = undefined when the browser closes the connection, which protects future calls to getDB(). However, any operations that have already resolved the dbp promise still hold a stale db reference and proceed to call db.transaction() at line 59 — which throws InvalidStateError on the now-closing connection. This can be triggered by: • The browser closing idle IDB connections (Safari is especially aggressive, but Chrome/Firefox do this too under memory pressure or tab suspension) • Another tab triggering a version upgrade (versionchange event — currently unhandled) • The verifyStoreExists path closing the DB (line 39) while concurrent operations still hold the old reference ### What changes do you think we should make in order to solve the problem? All changes are in lib/storage/providers/IDBKeyValProvider/createStore.ts: 1. Retry mechanism with a single retry on InvalidStateError    — Extract the transaction chain (getDB → verifyStoreExists → db.transaction) into openTransactionAndExecute()    — Wrap the call in .catch(): if the error is InvalidStateError, reset dbp = undefined and retry once with a fresh connection    — If the retry also fails, the error is thrown normally and still surfaces in Sentry exactly as it does today 2. Diagnostic logging on every retry (does NOT hide the issue)    — Log at alert level with: dbName, storeName, txMode, and closedBy    — closedBy tracks why the connection was closed: 'browser' (user-agent closed it), 'versionchange' (cross-tab upgrade), 'verifyStoreExists' (missing store path), or 'unknown'    — This gives us more visibility than today (where we only get a crash count with no context) 3. onversionchange handler    — When another tab triggers a DB version upgrade, proactively close the connection and reset dbp    — Without this, cross-tab upgrades can block indefinitely and in-flight operations crash on the stale connection 4. onclose handler now logs    — The existing onclose handler now logs when the browser unexpectedly closes the connection, giving us data on browser-initiated closures even when no retry is needed ### Key safety guarantees: • Data is never lost — the transaction fails before any data is written; the retry completes successfully with a valid connection • Only one retry is attempted — no infinite loops • If the retry fails, the error propagates normally and Sentry captures it as before • Normal operations (no InvalidStateError) are completely unaffected — zero performance impact • The closedBy logging gives us instrumentation to continue investigating the root cause of the spike cc @fabioh8010
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @Krishna2323

  • Upwork Job ID: 2029317821194916452

If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** 9.3.30-1 **Reproducible in staging?:** Y **Reproducible in production?:** Y **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** **Logs:** https://stackoverflow.com/c/expensify/questions/4856 **Expensify/Expensify Issue URL:** **Issue reported by:** @abbagail **Slack conversation** (hyperlinked to channel name): [#Expensify Bugs](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C049HHMV9SM/p1772579614206939) ## Action Performed: 1. Connect to NetSuite under Accounting 2. Do not enable tags in the workspace 3. Wait for auto-sync or trigger a manual sync 4. Receive a notification from AZ regarding tags 5. Open the Admin Chat link 6. Observe message: 7. “Preferred Exporter: Deleted all tags.” ## Expected Result: If tags are not enabled in the workspace, auto-sync should: Not attempt to delete tags. Not display a “Deleted all tags” message. title: [$250] Change `Add unreported expense` to `Add existing expense` in a report

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Daily
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Improvement
labels.description: Item broken or needs improvement.

labels.name: Help Wanted
labels.description: Apply this label when an issue is open to proposals by contributors

body:
Problem: when a new user goes through this very common flow: 1. Concierge helps with how to create an expense 2. User creates the expense, lands in the workspace chat 3. They then create a report via global create 4. Next they try to add the expense they just created However, they can't find it, and end up confused. Solution: - Change the menu item (and page title) from “Add unreported expenses” to “Add existing expenses” - Add the expense `Status` exposed filter right-aligned atop the RHP table with the values of the applicable states for this flow: “Unreported” and “Draft”. The default is not filtered by status. Note: "Select all" will be right-aligned and is already in motion via [this issue](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues/83741). - Don’t show expenses in the RHP list that are already on the draft report you actioned this flow from. Mock references:
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @sobitneupane

If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** **Reproducible in staging?:** Needs Reproduction (Unable to reproduce) **Reproducible in production?:** Needs Reproduction **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** **Logs:** https://stackoverflow.com/c/expensify/questions/4856 **Expensify/Expensify Issue URL:** **Issue reported by:** @Leeckhart **Slack conversation** (hyperlinked to channel name): [#Expensify Bugs](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C049HHMV9SM/p1772524049842419) ## Action Performed: 1. In New Expensify (NewDot), go to Reports. 2. Apply the following filters: Type: Expense Report, Status: Draft, Outstanding, Approved, Date: After December 31, 2025, From: Specific user 3. Observe the results list shows “Nothing to show.” 4. Change Type from Expense Report → Expense (keep all other filters the same) 5. Observe that expenses now appear in the list 6. In Classic (OldDot), go to Reports and apply equivalent filters: Date starting 2025-12-31, Statuses Draft / Outstanding / Approved, Same user 7. Observe that two matching Expense Reports appear. ## Expected Result: New Expensify should return the same two Expense Reports as Classic when the same filters are applied. ## Actual Result: New Expensify returns no results (“Nothing to show”) when Type = Expense Report is selected. Classic returns 2 matching reports with equivalent filters. Switching Type to Expense in New immediately shows results, indicating the issue is specific to Expense Report filtering. ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: Select the officially supported platforms where the issue was reproduced: - [ ] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [ ] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [x] Windows: Chrome - [x] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos The screenshots for the issue are in Slack [thread](https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C049HHMV9SM/p1772524049842419) [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

body:
If you haven’t already, check out our [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/Expensify/ReactNativeChat/blob/main/contributingGuides/CONTRIBUTING.md) for onboarding and email contributors@expensify.com to request to join our Slack channel! ___ **Version Number:** 9.3.16-4 **Reproducible in staging?:** Yes **Reproducible in production?:** Yes **If this was caught during regression testing, add the test name, ID and link from BrowserStack:** https://github.com/Expensify/App/pull/81530 **Email or phone of affected tester (no customers):** applausetester+ap3@applause.expensifail.com **Issue reported by:** Applause Internal Team **Bug source:** Pull Request QA execution **Device used:** IPhone 11 /IOS 26.1 / Samsung a31 **App Component:** Other ## Action Performed: 1.Open the iOS hybrid app 2.Sign in to New Expensify (NewDot) 3.Open the Troubleshoot section 4.Tap Go to Old Expensify (OldDot) 5.In OldDot, open the hamburger menu 6.Tap Try the new Expensify 7.Observe the landing screen ## Expected Result: The user lands on Home in New Expensify after returning from OldDot. ## Actual Result: The app briefly freezes and then redirects the user to Troubleshoot instead of Home. After some time, the app becomes responsive again, but the navigation destination is incorrect. This is an issue because the PR intent is to ensure users remain on Home by default, avoiding legacy or technical routes such as Troubleshoot. ## Workaround: Unknown ## Platforms: - [x] Android: App - [ ] Android: mWeb Chrome - [x] iOS: App - [ ] iOS: mWeb Safari - [ ] iOS: mWeb Chrome - [ ] Windows: Chrome - [ ] MacOS: Chrome / Safari ## Screenshots/Videos https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b1e868c8-bcd8-4f59-bc17-bb07ffab61e0 https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9d26db28-eb89-4b0f-8b97-9d7e0b20819b [View all open jobs on GitHub](https://github.com/Expensify/App/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+label%3A%22Help+Wanted%22)

title: [$250] Reduce forced focus mode switching

labels.name: Reviewing
labels.description: Has a PR in review

labels.name: External
labels.description: Added to denote the issue can be worked on by a contributor

labels.name: Weekly
labels.description: KSv2

labels.name: Melvin Triage
labels.description: Automatic AI Analysis of an issue

body:
Slack Thread Context: https://expensify.slack.com/archives/C05LX9D6E07/p1771328820064029?thread_ts=1771328820.064029&cid=C05LX9D6E07 **What the problem is:** Users are being automatically switched into `#focus mode` (an unread-only filter) in a way that feels jarring and confusing, causing chats to “disappear” from the Inbox during normal navigation. **What the expected output is:** - Reduce accidental/over-eager auto-switching by basing the threshold on the number of reports visible in the user’s LHN (not total reports in Onyx), so fewer users are forced into the mode. **Debugging info:** - Reference: https://fsty.io/v/Cw5YnkdF - Current logic described: auto-switch triggers when a user has **20+ reports in Onyx**, rather than **20+ reports in LHN**. - Example mentioned: user had **6 reports in LHN** but still got switched. - User impact reported: confusion that chats disappear after clicking into them and then navigating elsewhere; consistent negative feedback. **Potential solutions or implementation notes (if applicable):** - First step agreed: change threshold calculation to use **LHN report count** instead of **Onyx report count**.
Issue OwnerCurrent Issue Owner: @ShridharGoel
